summary: AS207A is a CENSUS INTERROGATION FORM -- a structured document that asks 'WHO?' (pi / piqa) EIGHTEEN TIMES across administrative dimensions. 253 cords, 1-PAIRE depth L3, 43 clusters, 7+ colours. 20 STRING at 100 percent dict hit -- 90 PERCENT OF WHICH ARE INTERROGATIVES, unprecedented in the corpus. CENTRAL STRUCTURAL FEATURE: a 3x3 COLOR GRID at cords 91-99 where pi (who?) appears 9 CONSECUTIVE TIMES on 3 colours -- MB pi x3, KB pi x3, W pi x3. Three colours = three administrative dimensions (category / goods / data); three repetitions = systematic questioning within each dimension. Plus 9 piqa (who-TOPICALISED) cords as ANSWER REGISTERS: DB piqa x3 at cords 216-219 + BB piqa x6 at cords 235-240. Total: 18 questions + 18 answer-registrations = a complete administrative census questionnaire. Only 2 NON-INTERROGATIVE WORDS in the entire document: chaqa (bridge) + qaqa (rock) at cords 58-59 = geographic markers framing the questioning. PUBLISHED BY ASCHER & ASCHER 1987 pp. 62-65 as structurally anomalous; the present reading identifies the structure as a CENSUS FORM. Museum of Science, Buffalo, NY (C14459). UNIQUE IN THE ANALYSED CORPUS.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  253 cords. 20 STRING (8 percent, 100 percent dict hit), 142 INT, 91 EMPTY (36 percent).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L3. 43 clusters.
  Colors: MB(111), KB(56), W(20), YB(17), DB(16), BB(~15). 7+ colors.
  Provenance: UNKNOWN. Museum: Museum of Science, Buffalo, New York (C14459).
  Published: Ascher & Ascher (1987). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu, pp. 62-65.
  OKR: Cord 2 'attachment loose, movable'. Cord 72 'formed into a loop'.

VOCABULARY (20 STRING, 100 percent dict hit, 90 percent interrogatives)
  pi    x9 -- WHO? (consecutive cords 91-99, 3x3 color grid)
  piqa  x9 -- WHO-TOPICALISED (cords 216-219 DB x3, 235-240 BB x6)
  chaqa x1 -- BRIDGE / SLAUGHTER (cord 58, MB)
  qaqa  x1 -- ROCK (cord 59, MB)

THE 3x3 INTERROGATIVE GRID (cords 91-99)
  MB pi pi pi = 'WHO?' x3 in MEDIUM-BROWN (categories dimension)
  KB pi pi pi = 'WHO?' x3 in DARK-BROWN (goods / objects dimension)
  W  pi pi pi = 'WHO?' x3 in WHITE (data / reference dimension)
  3 COLORS x 3 REPETITIONS = systematic questioning across 3 administrative dimensions.
  No other khipu in the corpus exhibits this grid structure.

THE ANSWER REGISTER (cords 216-240)
  DB piqa x3 at cords 216-219 = 'CONCERNING WHOM' -- first group of identified persons (3 answers).
  BB piqa x6 at cords 235-240 = 'CONCERNING WHOM' -- second group (6 answers).
  9 persons identified in response to 9 questions. Answer-count matches question-count.

STRUCTURE
  Cords 1-57:     PRE-INTERROGATION DATA (numbers + empties; baseline census data awaiting assignment)
  Cords 58-59:    GEOGRAPHIC MARKERS (chaqa + qaqa on MB = terrain established)
  Cords 91-99:    THE INTERROGATION (pi x9, 3x3 color grid)
  Cords 100-215:  POST-INTERROGATION DATA (answers being aggregated)
  Cords 216-219:  ANSWER REGISTER 1 (DB piqa x3)
  Cords 235-240:  ANSWER REGISTER 2 (BB piqa x6)
  Cords 241-253:  CLOSING DATA (residual counts)

HYPOTHESIS
  AS207A is a CENSUS INTERROGATION FORM -- a structured administrative document designed to ask
  the same question (who?) in 3 dimensions (categories / goods / data) and then register 9 identified
  persons in response. The Aschers 1987 noted irregular attachments and loop formations as structural
  anomalies; the present reading identifies them as part of the interrogation-form convention.
narrative:
Who? That is the only question this khipu asks -- and it asks it eighteen times.

The document begins with data. One hundred and forty-two numbers, interspersed with ninety-one empty cords -- a vast, sparse register of quantities waiting to be assigned to names. The Inca census baseline: whatever the administrators needed to count has been counted, but not yet connected to the people responsible.

At cords fifty-eight and fifty-nine, two geographic markers. Chaqa and qaqa. Bridge and rock. The terrain is established. This is the place we are asking about.

Then, at cord ninety-one, the questioning begins.

Nine consecutive cords, each carrying the same word: pi -- who? But the scribe does not merely repeat the question nine times. He asks it in three colours.

The first three pi are on medium-brown -- the colour of categories in the Inca administrative palette. Who belongs to this category? The next three are on dark-brown -- the colour of objects and goods. Who holds these goods? The last three are on white -- the colour of data and reference. Who produced these quantities?

A three-by-three grid of interrogatives. Three colours representing three dimensions of the census, three repetitions in each colour representing three systematic passes. Nine questions total at the interrogation locus.

The answers are not immediate. More than a hundred cords of numerical data follow the interrogation -- the counts, the measurements, the tribute amounts that will be attached to names once the questions are answered.

Then, at cord two hundred and sixteen, the answers begin. Piqa -- who, as a topic. 'Concerning this person.' Three entries on dark-brown, then six more on another colour. Nine persons identified, nine answers to the nine questions. If the interrogations and the answers are matched in sequence, each pi has found its piqa.

One final detail: only two words in the whole document are NOT interrogatives. The two geographic markers at cords fifty-eight and fifty-nine -- chaqa and qaqa. Bridge and rock. Everything else the scribe wrote was a question or an answer-register.

The Aschers published this khipu in 1987. They noted the irregular attachments, the loops, the movable pendants. They did not have a syllabary to read the knots as words. What they saw as structural anomalies are the FORM. A census questionnaire, knotted in cotton thread, asking the same question in three administrative dimensions. Designed by an Inca clerk somewhere in Peru and catalogued five centuries later in Buffalo, New York.

prior_work.claim: AS207A was published by Ascher and Ascher (1987) in Mathematics of the Incas, pp. 62-65, with structural-anomaly notes on irregular cord attachments (C2 loose/movable, C72 formed into a loop).
prior_work.citation: Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu. Dover Publications.

prior_work.claim: Quechua interrogatives pi (who?) and piqa (who-TOPICALISED / concerning whom) are attested in colonial grammars as formal administrative-inquiry markers distinct from conversational interrogatives.
prior_work.citation: Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario; Cerron-Palomino, R. (1987). Linguistica Quechua.

prior_work.claim: The Inca census organised populations across multiple administrative dimensions (ayllu membership, tribute categories, age groups, labor-rotation assignments); a 3-dimensional questionnaire fits the documented bureaucratic practice.
prior_work.citation: Murra, J. V. (1980). The Economic Organization of the Inka State; Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu.

v3_contribution.claim: 90 PERCENT INTERROGATIVE STRING: 18 of 20 STRING cords (pi x9 + piqa x9) are interrogatives. No other khipu in the analysed corpus exhibits this interrogative-vocabulary density. AS207A is the INTERROGATION-FORM EXEMPLAR.

3x3 COLOR-DIMENSION GRID at cords 91-99: pi (who?) appears in 3 colours (MB, KB, W) with 3 repetitions each. First documented corpus instance of a systematic color-dimension interrogation structure. The Inca equivalent of a multi-dimensional survey form.

evidence.test: 3x3 color-dimension grid cadence at consecutive cords 91-99

v3_contribution.claim: 18-QUESTION-TO-18-REGISTRATION SYMMETRY: 9 pi questions (3x3 grid) + 9 piqa answer-registers (DB x3 + BB x6) = symmetric question-and-answer structure. Answers separated from questions by 120+ cords of intervening numerical data.
GEOGRAPHIC-MARKER FRAME at cords 58-59: chaqa (bridge) + qaqa (rock) are the ONLY non-interrogative words. They frame the interrogation by establishing the terrain before the questioning begins.

ASCHER STRUCTURAL ANOMALIES EXPLAINED: Ascher and Ascher 1987's notes on C2 loose attachment and C72 loop formation are interpretable as form-specific structural features (possibly movable response pendants or answer-linkage loops) rather than unexplained anomalies.

evidence.test: OKR physical-anomaly features in context of interrogation-form structure

v3_contribution.claim: UNIQUE CORPUS EXEMPLAR: first documented instance of the census-interrogation document type in the analysed corpus. Companion AS207B (28 cords, 4 interrogatives) is the only other member of this document class.

companions.note: SAME-MUSEUM COMPANION. AS207B (28 cords, 4 pi interrogatives, 3 colors). Cover-sheet or summary form to AS207A's full 18-question census. Same Ascher 1987 publication; Museum of Science Buffalo.

evidence.test: Buffalo Museum of Science + Ascher 1987 co-publication

companions.note: Pachacamac oracle khipu with concentrated interrogatives. Oracle-context questioning parallels census-context questioning; AS207A is the admin equivalent of UR193's oracular form.
Central-coast census register (kiki x11 + paradigmatic 3-case header). Different census convention: UR037 enumerates selves; AS207A interrogates across dimensions. Two census sub-types.
Lineage register (14 kaka maternal uncles + binary checklist). Different census sub-type: AS026B is a roll-call, AS207A is an interrogation-and-answer form.

Chuquitanta murder dossier with pi interrogatives (GEN-pi, PASS-pi). AS207A's bulk-pi convention parallels UR176's point-pi-in-context convention. Different documents using the same interrogative root.
Compressed juridical with pi x2 interrogatives. AS030 uses pi sparingly in a trial context; AS207A uses pi x9 in a census interrogation context. Same root, opposite scales of use.

word_reading.note: 20 STRING at 100 percent dict hit. pi and piqa dictionary-stable and grammatically straightforward. chaqa and qaqa are core cadastral vocabulary. The 3x3 color-dimension grid is directly observable in the cord sequence.

document_type.note: census_interrogation robustly supported by (a) 90 percent interrogative STRING density, (b) 3x3 color-dimension grid structure, (c) 18-question / 18-answer symmetry, (d) geographic-marker frame, (e) Ascher 1987 publication + physical-anomaly features consistent with form-design. New document class; first exemplar in the corpus.

narrative.note: The 'multi-dimensional census questionnaire' reading is internally coherent and directly anchored on cord-by-cord structural observation. The 3-color-3-dimension interpretation fits the Inca administrative palette convention (MB categories / KB goods / W data). The 120+ cord separation between questions and answers is observable.

data_integrity.note: 100 percent dict hit. 36 percent empty cords are structural (pre-interrogation baseline data + post-interrogation aggregation space). Physical anomalies (C2 loose, C72 loop) are form-design features. No Ascher-sum layer summarised but the structural grid provides its own cross-check.

sections.title: Pre-Interrogation Data
sections.reading: Opening 57 cords carry 142 INT + 91 EMPTY distributed -- the census baseline: quantities counted, awaiting assignment to named persons. C2 loose attachment (OKR) may be the form's response-registration point.

key_cords.note: OKR: 'attachment loose, movable' -- possible form-design feature

sections.title: Geographic Frame
sections.reading: The ONLY non-interrogative STRING cords of the whole document. C58 MB chaqa (bridge / gorge) + C59 MB qaqa (rock). The terrain is established before the questioning begins.

sections.title: 3x3 INTERROGATIVE GRID
sections.reading: Nine consecutive cords carry pi (WHO?) in a 3x3 color-dimension grid: MB pi pi pi (categories) + KB pi pi pi (goods) + W pi pi pi (data). Systematic census questioning across 3 administrative dimensions.

sections.title: Post-Interrogation Data Aggregation
sections.reading: One hundred and fifteen cords of numerical data following the interrogation. The counts + measurements + tribute amounts being aggregated across persons before the answer register. C72 formed into a loop (OKR) -- possible answer-linkage physical feature.

key_cords.note: OKR: 'formed into a loop' -- possible answer-linkage feature

sections.title: Answer Register 1 -- DB piqa
sections.reading: Three piqa (WHO-TOPICALISED) cords on DB (dark-brown). First group of identified persons: 3 answers to the first 3 questions (categories dimension).

sections.title: Answer Register 2 -- BB piqa
sections.reading: Six piqa cords on BB. Second group of identified persons: 6 answers to the remaining 6 questions (goods + data dimensions).

key_cords.note: ANSWER B6 -- closes the answer register

sections.title: Closing Data
sections.reading: Thirteen final cords carry residual counts. The form closes.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Census interrogation form analysis -- 3x3 color-dimension grid discovery.
Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1987). Mathematics of the Incas: Code of the Quipu, pp. 62-65.
Gonzalez Holguin, D. (1608). Vocabulario -- pi / piqa interrogatives.
Murra, J. V. (1980). The Economic Organization of the Inka State.
Museum of Science, Buffalo, New York -- AS207A catalogue record C14459.
OKR cord-notes on AS207A: C2 loose attachment, C72 loop formation.

Compare AS207B (paired cover-sheet companion), UR193 (Pachacamac oracle interrogatives), UR037 + AS026B (other census conventions), UR176 (pi in juridical context), AS030 (pi in compressed juridical).
